title: Interfacial charge-induced adsorption mode for electron pairing in high-temperature superconductors
authors: Jiu Hui Wu, Hua Tian, Kejiang Zhou
abstract: The electron pairing mechanism by the interfacial charge-induced adsorption mode of high-temperature superconductors is revealed. For the YBCO superconductors, the coupling of electrons and valence-flexible state of oxygen ions forms a charge-regulated interfacial layer induced by the adsorption potential, and electrons are paired by sharing the optimized interfacial structure and exchanging the adsorption mode, generating strong attraction to form Cooper pairs. Then the effective interaction potential between electrons is exactly derived in details, as well as the electron-adsorption-mode coupling strength, in which the adsorption coupling constant is up to 43.4. Furthermore, we verify that d-wave come from the anisotropy of interfacial adsorption forces, and explain the pseudo-energy gap behavior. By using the one-dimensional Ginzburg-Landau equation in the absence of a magnetic field, we obtain the coherence length expression, and the coherence length calculated is very close to the literature results. By establishing the energy gap equation, we obtain the superconducting gap , which is very close to the measured result of 17meV by Scanning Tunneling Microscopy/Spectroscopy. These quantitative predictions close to the known results could verify our theoretical framework.
summary: This paper proposes a new mechanism for high-temperature superconductivity based on an interfacial adsorption mode between electrons and oxygen ions. By modeling the charge-regulated interface in YBCO, the authors provide a theoretical framework that quantitatively matches experimental measurements of the superconducting gap and symmetry. This approach offers a potential alternative to traditional strong-correlation models like RVB theory.
main_problem: The paper seeks to resolve the unresolved electron pairing mechanism in high-temperature superconductors (specifically cuprates like YBCO), including the origins of d-wave symmetry and the pseudogap phenomenon.
main_result: The authors propose an 'interfacial charge-induced adsorption mode' where electrons pair by exchanging adsorption modes, successfully predicting the superconducting gap (18.37 meV) and coherence length in close agreement with experimental STM/STS data.
method: The study employs a unified Hamiltonian with Bogoliubov linearization, Schrieffer-Wolff transformation, and the 1D Ginzburg-Landau equation to derive effective interaction potentials and superconducting properties.
model_or_system: The system is the YBCO superconductor, modeled as a charge-regulated interfacial layer where electrons are confined in an adsorption space between CuO2 layers, interacting with the valence-flexible state of oxygen ions.
key_observables: Superconducting gap (Delta), coherence length (xi), d-wave symmetry, and pseudogap behavior.
important_parameters: Adsorption coupling constant (up to 43.4), superconducting gap (18.37 meV), effective superconducting component ratio (omega approx 0.0306), and adsorption potential energy window (epsilon_p).
main_assumptions: The model assumes a charge-regulated interfacial layer induced by adsorption potential and uses the Bogoliubov linearization approximation for the chemical potential response.
paper_structure: The paper identifies the failure of BCS theory, proposes the adsorption potential framework, defines the microscopic Hamiltonian, derives the effective interaction and symmetry, and validates the theory through quantitative comparison with experimental literature.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
